mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances where unemployment crosses the 5.5% threshold over a 3-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real sales of autos and light trucks combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer spending power, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 3% alongside rising RSAFS, yielding a negative Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals immediate supply-side capacity utilization and inventory drawdowns, triggering short-term positive S&P 500 returns as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: Short-term returns turn negative during periods of industrial production acceleration when capacity utilization exceeds 85%, invalidating the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real gross domestic product reflect broad economic expansion and corporate revenue growth, consistently generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over three months.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth against 60-day forward returns falls below 0.02 across all rolling 5-year windows, indicating no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid monthly increases in the consumer price index trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in greater than 55% of cases following CPI spikes above the 0.4% monthly threshold, negating the tightening fear mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms genuine labor market tightening, driving positive S&P 500 returns as wage-driven consumption expectations rise.
failure_condition: The combined payroll and unemployment signal yields negative forward returns when real wage growth remains below 2% annually, breaking the consumption link.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp declines in housing starts indicate deteriorating construction sector demand and broader credit tightening, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines fail to predict negative short-term returns when mortgage rates fall concurrently by more than 50 basis points, offsetting the credit signal.
